modules.subject: math

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$716 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $20h + 14c = 716$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 20 in this context?
questions.questionText: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$716 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $20h + 14c = 716$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 20 in this context?

choices.text: The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her regular job
The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her second job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her regular job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her second job

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A marine biologist uses a linear model to estimate the weight of a blue whale after it is born. The model estimates that a certain blue whale weighs 5,710 pounds at birth and gains 10.0 pounds per hour, for 120 hours, after it is born. Based on this model, what is the estimated weight, in pounds, of this blue whale 7 hours after it is born?
questions.questionText: A marine biologist uses a linear model to estimate the weight of a blue whale after it is born. The model estimates that a certain blue whale weighs 5,710 pounds at birth and gains 10.0 pounds per hour, for 120 hours, after it is born. Based on this model, what is the estimated weight, in pounds, of this blue whale 7 hours after it is born?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: At a convention center, there are a total of 275 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is 0.68, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is 0.24. How many visitors are located in room C?

questions.prompt: The function $m$ is defined by $m(x) = 5x + 3$, and the function $p$ is defined by $p(x) = 3 - x$. What is the value of $2m(3) - p(3)$?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A school is ordering tablet computers for its students. The tablets cost \$170 each, and 7% tax is added to the total cost of the order. If the school can spend no more than \$3,400 on the tablets, including tax, what is the maximum number of tablets that the school can order?

questions.questionText: The difference between the measure of angle A and the measure of angle B is $-5\pi$ radians. Which expression shows the difference between the measure of angle A and the measure of angle B, in degrees?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 24% every 22.96 minutes. Which of the following equations could represent this model, where $M$ is the estimated mass, in grams, of the sample $t$ minutes after the researcher began observing the sample?
questions.questionText: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 24% every 22.96 minutes. questions.testName: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: For the right circular cone shown, $B$ is a point on the circumference of the base, and the length of segment $AB$ (not shown) is $84$ centimeters. If the height of the cone is $42$ centimeters and the volume of the cone is $k\pi$ cubic centimeters, what is the value of $k$?
questions.questionText: For the right circular cone shown, $B$ is a point on the circumference of the base, and the length of segment $AB$ (not shown) is $84$ centimeters. questions.testName: March 2025 International Form D SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In triangle $RST$, the measure of angle $R$ is $39^\circ$, the measure of angle $S$ is $x^\circ$, and the measure of angle $T$ is $(5x - 3)^\circ$. Point $L$ lies on $\overline{RS}$, point $K$ lies on $\overline{ST}$, and $\overline{LK}$ is parallel to $\overline{RT}$. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle $SKL$? (Disregard the degree symbol when entering your answer)

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Audrey's hair will grow at a constant rate from a length of 7 inches to a length of 11 inches. The equation $0.6t+7=11$ describes this situation, where t is the number of months it will take for Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 0.6 in this context?
questions.questionText: Audrey's hair will grow at a constant rate from a length of 7 inches to a length of 11 inches. The equation $0.6t+7=11$ describes this situation, where t is the number of months it will take for Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 0.6 in this context?

choices.text: The time, in months, it will take Audrey's hair to grow 1 inch
The length, in inches, Audrey's hair will grow each month
The time, in months, it will take Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches
The length, in inches, Audrey's hair will grow in 11 months

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A rectangle has a length that is 31 times its width. The function $y=(31w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y > 0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $31w$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A rectangle has a length that is 31 times its width. The function $y=(31w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y > 0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $31w$ in this context?

choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet
The area of the rectangle, in square feet
The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet
The width of the rectangle, in feet

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Audrey's hair will grow at a constant rate from a length of 7 inches to a length of 11 inches. The equation $0.6t+7=11$ describes this situation, where t is the number of months it will take for Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 0.6 in this context?
questions.questionText: Audrey's hair will grow at a constant rate from a length of 7 inches to a length of 11 inches. The equation $0.6t+7=11$ describes this situation, where t is the number of months it will take for Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 0.6 in this context?

choices.text: The time, in months, it will take Audrey's hair to grow 1 inch
The length, in inches, Audrey's hair will grow each month
The time, in months, it will take Audrey's hair to reach a length of 11 inches
The length, in inches, Audrey's hair will grow in 11 months

category.skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A rectangle has a length that is 31 times its width. The function $y=(31w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y > 0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $31w$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A rectangle has a length that is 31 times its width. The function $y=(31w)(w)$ represents this situation, where $y$ is the area, in square feet, of the rectangle and $y > 0$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $31w$ in this context?

choices.text: The length of the rectangle, in feet
The area of the rectangle, in square feet
The difference between the length and the width of the rectangle, in feet
The width of the rectangle, in feet
